children.label: A
children.text: involve and utilize a broad spectrum of community resources, including nonprofit community organizations, law enforcement organizations, and appropriate State and Federal agencies, including the State educational agencies;

children.label: B
children.text: attempt to relieve conditions that encourage crime; and

children.label: C
children.text: provide meaningful and lasting alternatives to involvement in crime.

children.heading: Consultation with the Ounce of Prevention Council
children.label: 2 Consultation with the Ounce of Prevention Council
children.text: The Attorney General may consult with the Ounce of Prevention Council in awarding grants under paragraph (1).
are innovative in approach to the prevention of crime in a specific area;
vary in approach to ensure that comparisons of different models may be made; and

coordinate crime prevention programs funded under this program with other existing Federal programs to address the overall needs of communities that benefit from grants received under this subchapter.
